data.title: Cathie Wood Highlights Bitcoin's Potential as 'Digital Gold'
data.description: PANews posted on X (formerly Twitter) that Cathie Wood, in ARK Invest's 'Big Ideas 2026', reiterated her core belief about Bitcoin: it is not only a risk asset but also possesses long-term hedging and value storage attributes. She emphasized that Bitcoin's correlation with gold throughout historical cycles is minimal, indicating it does not merely replicate gold's price logic. On the supply side, Bitcoin's advantage is more pronounced, as its issuance is mathematically capped at 21 million, unlike gold, which can be increasingly produced in response to price stimuli. This 'unacceleratable' scarcity gives Bitcoin the potential to become a significant value storage tool in future generational wealth transfers. According to Cathie Wood, the narrative of Bitcoin as digital gold is just beginning.

data.title: A major whale bought over 120,000 SOL tokens two hours ago at an average price of $84.57.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, whale AHdUMw spent 10.26 million USDC two hours ago to buy 121,368 SOL at an average price of $84.57.

data.title: Bitdeer has increased the size of its convertible senior notes offering to $325 million.
data.description: Nasdaq-listed Bitcoin mining company Bitdeer announced it will increase the size of its convertible senior notes offering to $325 million. Bitdeer also plans to grant initial purchasers an option to purchase up to an additional $50 million of the notes within 13 days of the initial offering date (inclusive). (Globenewswire)

data.title: The author of "Rich Dad Poor Dad" has bought Bitcoin at a price of $67,000, and the US dollar may be impacted by the debt crisis.
data.description: Robert Kiyosaki, author of "Rich Dad Poor Dad," posted on the X platform that despite the ongoing downturn in the crypto market, he chose to increase his holdings and bought Bitcoin at $67,000 for two reasons: 1. When the US debt problem impacts the dollar system, "massive money printing" will begin, with the Federal Reserve again printing trillions of dollars in fiat currency; 2. The total supply of Bitcoin, capped at 21 million, is nearing full mining, and when the "21 millionth Bitcoin" is mined, Bitcoin will have an advantage over gold.

data.title: MARA Completes Acquisition of Exaion Stake to Boost European Digital Infrastructure
data.description:
MARA announced on Friday that its French subsidiary, MARA France, has finalized the acquisition of a 64% stake in Exaion. According to ChainCatcher, the agreement was initially reached in August of last year. As part of the transaction, NJJ Capital has formed a strategic partnership with MARA, acquiring a 10% stake in MARA France. EDF Group remains a minority shareholder and client of Exaion.

Market reports indicate that MARA purchased the controlling stake in Exaion for $168 million in cash from its former parent company, EDF Pulse Ventures. The French government reviewed the transaction due to concerns over national sovereignty. The Exaion board will consist of representatives appointed by MARA, EDF Pulse Ventures, NJJ Capital, and Exaion's CEO and co-founder. Billionaire Xavier Niel and MARA CEO Fred Thiel will join the Exaion board.

The collaboration aims to accelerate Exaion's expansion and enhance its capabilities in secure cloud services and high-performance computing, positioning the France-based company as a leader in European digital infrastructure. EDF Group provides energy and services to approximately 41 million customers, with projected consolidated sales of €113.3 billion in 2025. MARA, listed on Nasdaq, saw its stock price decline by about 40% last year and is expected to release its quarterly earnings next week. In the previous quarter, the company achieved a record profit of $123 million by combining its Bitcoin operations with new power and AI assets.

data.title: US stocks closed mixed in the crypto sector, with Coinbase rising 3.26%.
data.description: According to data from msx.com, at the close of trading on the US stock market, the Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 0.47%, the S&P 500 rose 0.69%, and the Nasdaq Composite rose 0.90%. The crypto sector was mixed, with Bit Digital down 2.38%, ETHZilla down 1.37%, Circle up 1.78%, Robinhood up 0.61%, and Coinbase up 3.26%. msx.com is a decentralized RWA trading platform that has listed hundreds of RWA tokens, covering US stock and ETF tokens such as AAPL, AMZN, GOOGL, META, MSFT, NFLX, and NVDA.

data.title: U.S. Tariffs move to 10% after Supreme Court IEEPA curb
data.description:
After the Supreme Court curbed IEEPA tariffs, the administration used Section 122 to set a 10% global tariff; analysts cite higher costs and refund steps.
source: https://coincu.com/news/u-s-tariffs-move-to-10-after-supreme-court-ieepa-curb/

data.title: SpaceX Plans to Exceed 10,000 Satellites in Orbit Next Month
data.description: A recent post by a user suggested that SpaceX could surpass 10,000 Starlink satellites in orbit as early as next month. According to Jin10, Elon Musk responded by stating that the Starship will launch over 10,000 satellites annually.

data.title: Bitmine's associated address withdrew another 17,200 ETH from Kraken.
data.description:
According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a newly created wallet “0x1AE” associated with Bitmine has received another 17,222 ETH from Kraken, worth $34.7 million.

The wallet currently holds a total of 27,722 ETH, with a total value of approximately $54.57 million.

data.title: Ethereum’s hard turn signals rising pressure from high-performance rivals
data.description:
Ethereum is upgrading its base layer with FOCIL, account abstraction, and other architectural changes to improve security and censorship resistance.
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/ethereums-hard-turn-signals-rising-pressure/

data.title: Sony's Acquisition Strategy Emphasizes Private Equity and Diverse Funding
data.description: Sony's Chief Executive Officer Hiroki Totoki highlighted the importance of utilizing private equity and various funding sources beyond conventional bank loans and stock for the company's acquisitions. Bloomberg posted on X, emphasizing that this approach is central to Sony's strategic growth and expansion plans. Totoki's remarks underscore the company's commitment to leveraging diverse financial avenues to enhance its competitive edge in the market.

data.title: Global Liquidations Reach $182 Million in 24 Hours
data.description:
In the past 24 hours, global liquidations have totaled $182 million, according to ChainCatcher. Data from Coinglass reveals that long positions accounted for $72.5 million, while short positions amounted to $109 million. Specifically, Bitcoin long positions saw liquidations of $24.5 million, and Bitcoin short positions experienced $45.3 million in liquidations. Ethereum long positions faced $17.5 million in liquidations, with Ethereum short positions reaching $18.1 million.

Additionally, over the last 24 hours, 80,609 individuals globally have been liquidated. The largest single liquidation occurred on Bybit's BTCUSDT pair, valued at $2.03 million.

data.title: Coinbase draws AML focus amid Sokolovski bail denial
data.description:
Reports show Sokolovski denied bail; no verified admission of laundering $100M via Coinbase; DOJ/NYDFS crypto money laundering regulatory oversight focus.
source: https://coincu.com/news/coinbase-draws-aml-focus-amid-sokolovski-bail-denial/

data.title: Tether to Cease Support for CNHT Product
data.description:
Tether has announced a strategic adjustment to its CNHT product, ceasing support to better focus on community-driven core products. According to PANews, Tether will halt all new CNHT issuances and stop minting additional tokens effective immediately. A year after the announcement, redemption support for CNHT will end, with a reminder notification to be issued before the redemption deadline.

Tether stated that the decision was influenced by changes in market conditions, low product interest, and limited ongoing demand from the community compared to other assets. The current usage scale of CNHT is insufficient to justify the operational costs required to maintain the same standards as other products.

Until the redemption deadline, Tether will continue to process CNHT redemption requests in accordance with its terms of service. The company advises users holding CNHT on supported blockchain networks to complete redemption operations promptly.

data.title: Altcoin Season Index Declines as Bitcoin Outperforms
data.description: The Altcoin Season Index, as reported by CoinMarketCap, has decreased by two points to 33, indicating a phase where Bitcoin is outperforming many leading altcoins. According to NS3.AI, this index evaluates the 90-day performance of the top 100 cryptocurrencies in comparison to Bitcoin to assess market sentiment. An altcoin season is typically identified when 75% of these altcoins surpass Bitcoin's performance, suggesting that the current market conditions are not strongly in favor of altcoins.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | Automakers Seek Protection from New Tariffs After Supreme Court Decision
data.description: Detroit's automakers have requested intervention from the White House to protect them from new tariffs that U.S. President Donald Trump has pledged to implement. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the automakers' concerns following the Supreme Court's decision to overturn the majority of Trump's global duties. The automakers are seeking relief as they face potential financial impacts from the proposed tariffs, which could affect their operations and competitiveness in the global market. The industry is closely monitoring the situation, hoping for favorable outcomes that would mitigate the economic challenges posed by the new tariffs.

data.title: Institutional Investors Record Significant Equity Sales Amid Retail and Hedge Fund Purchases
data.description:
Institutional investors sold a net total of $8.3 billion in U.S. equities last week, marking the second-largest weekly sale on record. In contrast, retail investors continued their buying streak, purchasing $1.0 billion worth of equities, marking their fifth consecutive week of net purchases. Hedge funds also increased their holdings, buying $1.2 billion, which is their eighth purchase in the last nine weeks, The Kobeissi Letter posted on X.

Overall, equity exchange-traded funds (ETFs) experienced inflows of $2.2 billion last week. However, single stocks faced significant outflows, amounting to $8.3 billion. This trend of outflows from single stocks has persisted for 13 of the last 15 weeks, with a cumulative total of $52.0 billion.

The data indicates a pattern where institutions are aggressively selling equities to both retail investors and hedge funds.

data.title: Venezuela and Colombia Discuss Natural Gas Imports Amid Shortfall
data.description: Senior officials from Venezuela and Colombia convened in Caracas to discuss natural gas imports. Bloomberg posted on X that the meeting comes as Colombia faces a growing shortfall in natural gas supply. The Colombian government, led by outgoing President Gustavo Petro, is actively seeking solutions to address this energy challenge. The discussions are part of broader efforts to strengthen energy cooperation between the two neighboring countries. Both nations are exploring ways to enhance their energy security and ensure a stable supply of natural gas to meet domestic demands.

data.title: Ethereum weighs RISC-V layer amid censorship push
data.description:
Ethereum is weighing a RISC-V execution layer to bolster censorship resistance; analysts cite EVM and zkEVM trade-offs and liability in FOCIL (EIP-7805).
source: https://coincu.com/news/ethereum-weighs-risc-v-layer-amid-censorship-push/

data.title: Animoca Brands Receives Dubai VASP License to Offer Institutional Services
data.description:
Animoca Brands secures a Dubai VASP licence, enabling regulated broker-dealer and investment services for institutional and qualified investors while expanding Middle East operations.
source: https://catenaa.com/markets/cryptocurrencies/animoca-brands-receives-dubai-vasp-license-to-offer-institutional-services/
